title: knautia purpurea subsp. subscaposa
intro: Knautia purpurea subsp. subscaposa, commonly known as shepherd's needle, is a perennial plant belonging to the Caprifoliaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. The health benefits of Knautia purpurea subsp. subscaposa include soothing digestive issues, alleviating menstrual cramps, and reducing respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, often in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
